steps.title: Identify the right angle and tangent components
steps.content: Since angles A and B are acute, angle C must be 90°. From angle B in this right triangle: opposite = AC = 23.3, adjacent = BC = unknown.

steps.title: Use the tangent ratio to solve for BC
steps.content: We know $\tan B = \frac{1}{4}$. By the tangent ratio in a right triangle, $\tan B = \frac{AC}{BC}$. Therefore: $$\frac{23.3}{BC} = \frac{1}{4}$$ Multiply both sides by BC and by 4: $$BC = 23.3 \times 4 = 93.2$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
